[2018-04-01 09:22] iminabearsuit: Need raid backs. Need to have something to blame for my ineptitude.
[2018-04-01 09:23] p0lari (Polari): reminder that dota started out as a custom map people played to mess around in when wc3 ladder got too stressful
[2018-04-01 09:24] p0lari (Polari): it's a fine game even if you have no clue what you're doing as long as everyone else in the game is the same
[2018-04-01 09:25] p0lari (Polari): I have trouble picturing how to compare how "hard" different competitive games are
[2018-04-01 09:25] p0lari (Polari): like, they are as hard as the opponents you're playing against?
[2018-04-01 09:26] p0lari (Polari): I can kinda imagine where that sentiment might be coming from, since in prismata you have nothing to blame but yourself (1v1, almost nonexistent rng) and you get rekt pretty quickly when you're starting out
[2018-04-01 09:26] mqp: it's not hard to measure performance in competitive games with respect to perfect play in many situations
[2018-04-01 09:27] mqp: and if you can measure performance you can talk about learning curve and performance as a function of time and the performance distribution
[2018-04-01 09:27] iminabearsuit: Skill ceiling, and how much advantage a high skill level will give you, is usually how to judge how hard a game is.
[2018-04-01 09:28] p0lari (Polari): ok so now we have two slightly different, academic definitions for the "difficulty" of a competitive game
[2018-04-01 09:29] p0lari (Polari): do you think if some kid who's trying out prismata says it's hard he means one of those definitions?
[2018-04-01 09:30] mqp: i think what he's meaning is that he can see that he makes a lot of mistakes (i.e. he is early on the learning curve) and he doesn't know how to fix them immediately (i.e. performance goes up slowly over time) and other players crush him (i.e. he is way on the left of the distribution)
[2018-04-01 09:30] mqp: i wouldn't have said it if i didn't think he meant that
[2018-04-01 09:31] p0lari (Polari): ok so now you're mostly talking about how easy it is to notice your mistakes, which you didn't touch at all when you previously defined the meaning
[2018-04-01 09:32] mqp: no, i think even if you can't notice your mistakes individually it still feels hard if your opponents crush you and you don't know how to do any better
[2018-04-01 09:32] p0lari (Polari): so the difficulty of a game is a function of the playerbase?
[2018-04-01 09:32] p0lari (Polari): so like, if you take dota 10 years ago and the same patch today, it's a harder game now?
[2018-04-01 09:33] mqp: well, if you suppose that some game exists where all humans are terrible and nobody recognizes that there is any way to play better, then i think all the humans in that game will perceive that it's "not as hard" as it "really is"
[2018-04-01 09:33] mqp: but i don't know an example of a game that actually fits that description, usually you can see that it's possible to play better and usually some humans are much better than others
[2018-04-01 09:34] p0lari (Polari): basically any game early on vs late in its lifespan?
[2018-04-01 09:34] mqp: i definitely think that new players entering dota will experience it as a harder game now for that reason, although maybe it got easier for other reasons (e.g. better instructional material)
[2018-04-01 09:36] p0lari (Polari): I notice you're now phrasing this as "will experience it as a harder game" rather than talking about the difficulty as a property of the game
[2018-04-01 09:36] iminabearsuit: I would for sure say that Dota is harder now than before, due to the elevated skill ceiling of competitive player.
[2018-04-01 09:36] iminabearsuit: Because you have someone to compare yourself to.
[2018-04-01 09:37] mqp: but i think "will experience as a harder game" is highly correlated with the properties of the game i said
[2018-04-01 09:37] p0lari (Polari): so it got harder even if nothing about the game itself changed?
[2018-04-01 09:37] iminabearsuit: Like when I was 12 years old, I didn't consider Warcraft 3 a difficult game. But now I do, because I know the skill ceiling of it.
[2018-04-01 09:38] mqp: to be presented with players playing much better than you in unreachable ways, that is mostly caused when the game lends itself to deep, subtle gameplay that has a lot of things that are hard for humans to learn
[2018-04-01 09:38] extratricky: I think if people were considering the skill ceiling of rts/moba they would say those games are harder than prismata
[2018-04-01 09:38] p0lari (Polari): so we're now saying the difficulty of a game is a subjective measure?
[2018-04-01 09:39] iminabearsuit: The word hard is subjective by default.
[2018-04-01 09:39] mqp: i considered warcraft 3 as a difficult as fuck game when i was 12 so i guess bearsuit and me are different
[2018-04-01 09:39] iminabearsuit: Well, I was happy just having fun and playing with friends.
[2018-04-01 09:40] mqp: it basically seemed hard enough that i played custom games instead of making an effort to learn it
[2018-04-01 09:41] iminabearsuit: Playing through the campaign learnt you all the mechanics to play by yourself.
